name: Ra
shortDescription: Ra is the ancient Egyptian sun god, a central deity in the Egyptian pantheon. He was believed to travel across the sky each day in his solar barque and navigate the underworld each night.
description: Ra, also known as Re, is the ancient Egyptian sun god and one of the most important deities in the Egyptian pantheon. He was considered the creator of the world and the source of all life. Ra was believed to travel across the sky each day in his solar barque, the Barque of Millions of Years, bringing light and warmth to the world. At sunset, he would enter the underworld, Duat, where he would face numerous challenges and battles against the serpent Apophis (Apep) to ensure the sun's return the next morning. This nightly journey symbolized the cycle of death and rebirth. Ra was often depicted as a falcon-headed man wearing a solar disk encircled by a uraeus (cobra), representing his royal authority and divine power. He was associated with kingship, order, and the maintenance of cosmic balance (Ma'at). Temples dedicated to Ra were centers of worship and power, and his cult flourished throughout Egyptian history. Different forms and aspects of Ra were recognized, such as Atum-Ra (Ra as the evening sun and creator) and Khepri (Ra as the rising sun). The Eye of Ra was a powerful symbol, representing his feminine counterpart and his protective force. The worship of Ra was deeply intertwined with Egyptian cosmology and beliefs about the afterlife, with his daily journey serving as a metaphor for the individual's journey through life and death.

sources.title: Ancient Egyptian Literature, Volume I: The Old and Middle Kingdoms
sources.author: Miriam Lichtheim
